title: Container signing against an HSM.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

** Container Signing against an HSM (Hardware Security Module) **

According to docs/tutorials/image_signing.md, the private key needs to be on the same server as the signing server. In my infrastructure this is not possible as the private key is protected by an HSM. Is it possible to either (a) produce a document that outlines the format of a signed container so that we can write the code ourselves to produce the same format that podman expects or (b) provide hook points in the signing process to send the hash(es) for signing and expect a signature back.

I prefer option (a)

**Describe the results you received:**

Does not look like it is possible today according to image_signing.md.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The ability to sign a container against a private key held on an HSM.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes but found nothing mentioning how to do what i want to do.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: I'll tag @mtrmac and @vrothberg given their work on cosign

a) https://github.com/containers/image/tree/main/docs 

b) see containers/container-libs#268 .

Cosign would entirely change a); as for b), the supportability/testing concerns would stay the same, and continue to likely be a blocker; the _technology_ is very different (quite likely impacting which HSMs are usable).
Any chance Cosign would let us delegate the HSM bits to their upstream? Or was that too much to hope?
Regardless, closing as dupe of containers/container-libs#268

> Any chance Cosign would let us delegate the HSM bits to their upstream? Or was that too much to hope?

To an extent. There is quite a lot of various interoperability code there (including _some_ PKCS#11 support); but most of that is something we can’t practically test, so it’s unclear that we would want to ship it in Podman. It’s definitely not going to be a part of our _first_ version.

Cosign could help in the sense that it would be possible to use Podman to create an image, and then use the the upstream `cosign` utility to sign images (**assuming they are on a trusted storage mechanism (e.g. a registry) between creating and signing**) — and the two are expected to be interoperable.
